metadata.description: Deploy PIM Manager to Azure Static Web Apps
Name of the Azure Static Web App resource
App Registration Client ID from Microsoft Entra ID

properties.scriptContent:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# azure-cli >= 2.64 ships on Azure Linux (CBL-Mariner, glibc) with the tdnf package manager.
# StaticSitesClient (the binary behind `swa deploy`) is a self-contained .NET binary. Beyond ICU
# it needs the rest of the .NET native runtime dependencies (krb5, openssl-libs, zlib) for its
# networking/TLS during "Preparing deployment"; without them it fails with "An unknown exception
# has occurred" / "missing shared libraries" / exit code 1 even though ICU alone is installed.
# Install Node.js/npm plus the full .NET native dep set, unzip and curl so the binary can execute.
tdnf install -y nodejs npm icu krb5 openssl-libs zlib unzip curl ca-certificates

# Install SWA CLI
npm install -g @azure/static-web-apps-cli@2 --silent

# Download latest release
curl -sL https://github.com/0125joel/PIM-manager/releases/latest/download/release.zip -o release.zip
unzip -q release.zip

# Inject Client ID into runtime config
printf 'window.__PIM_CONFIG__ = { clientId: "%s" };\n' "$CLIENT_ID" > out/env-config.js

# Run StaticSitesClient under globalization-invariant mode. The swa deploy binary is a .NET app;
# if the azure-cli image's ICU version does not match what it expects, it dies at "Preparing
# deployment" with a generic "An unknown exception has occurred". Invariant mode bypasses ICU for
# the deploy CLI only (it does not affect the deployed site).
export DOTNET_SYSTEM_GLOBALIZATION_INVARIANT=1

# Deploy to Azure Static Web Apps. The app and this script are created in the same ARM
# deployment; the SWA's content/deployment endpoint provisions asynchronously, so for a short
# window right after creation the deployment token is rejected with "No matching Static Web App
# was found or the api key was invalid". Retry with a delay until the endpoint is ready.
deployed=0
for attempt in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do
  echo "swa deploy attempt $attempt..."
  if swa deploy ./out --deployment-token "$DEPLOYMENT_TOKEN" --env production; then
    deployed=1
    break
  fi
  echo "attempt $attempt failed; the SWA endpoint may not be ready yet, waiting 30s..."
  sleep 30
done

if [ "$deployed" -ne 1 ]; then
  echo "=== swa deploy still failing after retries; diagnosing StaticSitesClient ==="
  swa deploy ./out --deployment-token "$DEPLOYMENT_TOKEN" --env production --verbose=silly || true
  BIN=$(find /root/.swa -name StaticSitesClient -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  echo "StaticSitesClient path: $BIN"
  if [ -n "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "--- ldd ---"; ldd "$BIN" || true
  fi
  exit 1
fi
